steps.title: Compare the requested drawbacks
steps.content: The student wants to compare disadvantages of docked and dockless bike-share programs. The notes identify a drawback for each type: docked systems require significant space and money, while dockless systems can be disorganized.

steps.title: Eliminate mismatched choices
steps.content: Choice A pairs a dockless disadvantage with a docked advantage, so it does not compare two disadvantages. Choice B mentions that dockless programs are easy and inexpensive to implement, which is an advantage, and it also misstates the notes by saying dockless programs are less flexible. Choice D gives a dockless advantage and then a general fact about bike-share programs.

steps.title: Use the sentence with both issues
steps.content: The remaining sentence identifies docked programs as more resource-intensive, which reflects the space and money requirement. It also refers to the organizational challenges of dockless programs. That directly compares one disadvantage of each program type. The answer is C.
